briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to today's climate briefing on May 15, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis, A Global Warning
sections.content: International news gathered today warns that climate change is intensifying globally. Scientists have reported that climate change is reducing oxygen levels in rivers worldwide [21, 28, 29, 32, 36, 37], which could pose a severe threat to ecosystems and human health. Furthermore, it has been confirmed that climate change is weakening social connections, leading to casualties [26, 44], and having widespread impacts ranging from crop threats in Colorado [20] to water issues in New York City [30] and economic and security threats in Nigeria [43]. The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) warns that climate change could push one million Ghanaians into poverty by 2050 [49]. Despite many cities worldwide preparing for climate change [27], there are also observations that this crisis remains an 'invisible crisis' that is not being adequately addressed [47]. These warnings, coupled with extreme weather events [42], clearly demonstrate that the climate crisis is no longer a distant future concern.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Spreading Domestic Climate Action and Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, with the confirmation of the Presidential Committee on the National Climate Crisis Response [1], climate actions and carbon neutrality efforts by various stakeholders are actively underway. The Incheon Institute is strengthening international cooperation for climate change response [2], and Gunwi County is proactively responding to heatwaves by introducing a cooling fog system [3]. Various local governments, including Gwangyang City and Geoje City, are embedding carbon neutrality into daily life through initiatives such as using reusable containers, recycling discarded banners, and public awareness campaigns [4, 9]. Furthermore, comprehensive efforts spanning public institutions, corporations, and schools are continuing, including ESG tree planting by the National Hoengseong Forest Recreation Center and Hansung Motors [5, 11], carbon neutrality lifestyle practice campaigns by Namdong Power and Tangjeong Elementary School [6, 7], and a used clothing donation campaign by KEPCO MCS Chungbuk Branch [10]. In particular, Korea Midland Power is pursuing a 'Public-led Industrial Complex Solar Power Project' to achieve carbon neutrality in industrial complexes [8], exploring concrete, region-based implementation strategies.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is emerging as a crucial means of strengthening energy security both domestically and internationally [16], and concrete movements to accelerate this transition are observed within the country. Gyeonggi Province is playing a leading role by launching the 'Gyeonggi Energy Transition Working Group' and establishing a strategy to secure 10GW of renewable energy by 2030 [13, 15, 19]. The Yeoju Haetbitdure Village Solar Power Project is regarded as an important success story in energy transition [17], and private sector participation is also active, with the establishment of the renewable energy company 'ECO CORE NEXT' in Seonggong-ri Village [12]. Korea Southern Power is also making efforts to expand renewable energy supply by signing a business agreement with the Korea Industrial Complex Corporation and Shinhan Asset Management to vitalize industrial complex solar power [18]. However, news that the Gwangju and Jeonnam regions, where renewable energy generation is active, are facing difficulties in expanding renewable energy due to grid deficiencies [14] suggests that infrastructure challenges remain to be addressed in the energy transition process.

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Response Strategies and Legal Disputes
sections.content: The international community is exploring various strategies and approaches to address climate change. In the United States, climate litigation is actively unfolding, with lawsuits being filed against 'the biggest attack ever' on climate change [23, 39], and there are calls for Virginia to consider legislative measures addressing issues such as methane [24]. The European Central Bank (ECB) is integrating climate change into its monetary policy strategy [50], leading changes in the financial system. ArcelorMittal Mining Canada has also pledged to invest over $400 million to resolve issues through an agreement with Environment and Climate Change Canada [31, 46]. Amidst ongoing diverse research and discussions on topics such as the impact of climate change on social health [25], its effect on flood occurrences [34], and the predictability of Artificial Intelligence (AI) [38], the news that the UK recently lost its status as the largest contributor to the UN Green Climate Fund following aid cuts [54] indicates a shift in international climate finance flows.

briefing.closing: Through today's briefing, we have examined the escalating reality of the climate crisis and the diverse efforts being made both domestically and internationally to address it. We must continue to foster a climate-resilient future through sustained attention and cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: Ban private jets and cut speed limits to avert UK fuel crisis, say campaigners
articles.description: Climate and transport organisations warn ministers not to ‘sleepwalk into crisis’ amid Iran war oil and gas shortagesPrivate jets should be banned and the speed limit on UK motorways reduced to 60mph as part of a pre-emptive effort to ease the looming fuel supply crisis, according to leading climate and transport organisations.The group – including Greenpeace and Transport and Environment – are calling on ministers not to “sleepwalk into a crisis” that could lead to severe shortages of jet fuel

summary.phenomenon: A warning has been issued in the UK regarding a potential impending fuel supply crisis. Specific details on the timing or scale were not mentioned in the article.
summary.cause: Oil and gas shortages due to the Iran war were identified as the cause of the crisis.
summary.outlook: Climate and transport organizations proposed banning private jets and reducing the speed limit on UK motorways to 60mph, warning that a failure to implement such measures could lead to severe jet fuel shortages.
